hypothesis: Routing chat-short-shaped traffic through infergate (via-gateway/gateway-mock) instead of directly at the engine (engine-direct) adds non-queue latency overhead whose pooled p95 is <10ms and pooled p99 is <20ms (the program SLO target, docs/tasks.md IB-T010 hypothesis (a)), falsifiable against LiteLLM's self-reported ~8ms p95 gateway overhead as a source-reported baseline (as of 2026-07 -- re-verify at use time). The expected direction is 'gateway adds a small, bounded amount of latency, not zero and not an order of magnitude more'; the run is designed at low offered rate relative to estimated capacity so queueing delay is negligible and the measured delta approximates non-queue overhead.
expected_direction: via-gateway/gateway-mock pooled client TTFT p95/p99 higher than engine-direct by a small bounded margin (target: p95 <10ms, p99 <20ms); gateway-side inference_ttft_seconds histogram (recv-to-first-upstream-byte) should show the same small addition once network/client scheduling noise is accounted for
workload: ib-t010-e1-mock@1.0.0 (seed 10010101) for the mock sub-experiment; ib-t010-e1-llamacpp@1.0.0 (seed 10010102) for the llama.cpp sub-experiment
constants: engine (mock-backend: same seed/ttft=300ms/itl/error-rate=0, OR llama.cpp llama-server: same model checkpoint+commit, same -np/-c/-t flags), model checkpoint+revision, hardware (4-core CPU local-dev-container, no GPU), workload version+seed, warm-up policy, offered rate (held well below estimated capacity so queueing stays negligible) all held fixed across the direct/gateway pair within each sub-experiment; ONLY target_topology (and the gateway block its presence structurally implies, internal/manifest.ImpliedFields) differs between the direct and gateway arms of a given sub-experiment. The mock sub-experiment and the llama.cpp sub-experiment are two SEPARATE single-variable comparisons (different engine == different constants block), not a joint 2x2 -- each is executed as its own `experiment compare` invocation reusing this same hypothesis file (same declared variable, same falsifiable claim, tested against two backend types).
stop_condition: each sub-experiment (mock, llama.cpp) completes >= 3 repetitions per arm (direct, gateway) at the declared low offered rate, or the target becomes unreachable (typed target_unreachable abort) or the schedule-slip watchdog fires (client-host-as-bottleneck abort)
repeat_policy: >= 3 runs per arm per sub-experiment, pooled percentiles across repetitions (never averaged), median +/- range across runs reported alongside the pooled figure
slo_reference: docs/evidence/ib-t010/e1-mock.slo.json (model-serving scope, ttft_seconds, measured) and docs/evidence/ib-t010/e1-llamacpp.slo.json (model-serving scope, ttft_seconds, measured); the gateway-overhead SLO itself (p95<10ms/p99<20ms) is a program target evaluated directly against the pooled TTFT delta computed from the two arms' benchmark-result files, not a per-request goodput signal (gateway_overhead_non_queue_seconds is a cross-arm derived quantity, not a raw per-request event field, so it is reported alongside goodput@SLO rather than through it)
provenance_notes: LiteLLM self-reports ~8ms p95 added latency in its own benchmarking material (source-reported, as of 2026-07 -- re-verify the current figure at use time; provenance=source-reported, not independently measured by this program). Program SLO target (p95<10ms, p99<20ms) is a project-declared target (provenance=assumed/program-target), recorded in docs/tasks.md IB-T010 and docs/experiments.md Set 1. This is the REAL IB-T010 published claim -- distinct from EXP-ib-t009-gateway-overhead-demo.json, which was IB-T009's governance-mechanics smoke test (1 rep/arm, no external baseline falsified).
